onboarding.welcomeBody: This tool reads one SEC 10-K filing and locates every numbered Item inside it, with a confidence score and checks for each. Here is a 30-second tour.
onboarding.taglineBody: It indexes Items into the source bytes — it never rewrites text — and gives each one a calibrated confidence. Hover any ⓘ for a plain definition of a term.
onboarding.demoBody: Pick a pre-selected filing here to load a real example with no token. Once it loads, the tour continues with how to read the report.
onboarding.lookupTitle: Or look up your own filing
onboarding.lookupBody: Enter a ticker + fiscal year, an EDGAR accession, or paste raw filing text. This needs an access token from the operator.
onboarding.tabsBody: Inspect examines one filing item-by-item. Eval / still-hard is the aggregate quality report across many filings.
onboarding.langBody: Switch between English and 繁體中文 anytime; your choice is remembered.
onboarding.loadExampleTitle: Now let's read a real report
onboarding.loadExampleBody: Click Next and I'll load a sample filing (Microsoft, 1995), then walk you through how to read its result.
onboarding.metaBody: Company, form type, fiscal year, accession, and the format era of the document you just loaded.
onboarding.navBody: Every Item in the filing. The colored dot is the verdict: green = high confidence, amber = medium, red = low or a boundary problem, grey = legitimately absent. Click any Item to inspect it.
onboarding.detailBody: The badges show the status, the calibrated confidence (with its score), and the exact character range this Item occupies in the source.
onboarding.boundaryTitle: Proof it was indexed, not generated
onboarding.boundaryBody: The highlighted span is the Item's text inside the original document — copied by position, never rewritten. This is what "index, don't generate" means.
onboarding.provBody: The checks this Item passed (✓) or failed (✕). A failed check, like boundary_xcheck, is what turns an Item red and flags it for review.
onboarding.summaryTitle: How to read the filing summary
onboarding.summaryBody: Filing-level health: coverage, how many Items are present / legitimately absent / extraction failures, and a needs-review verdict with its reasons. The Item 8 XBRL oracle and escalation tier are independent cross-checks.
onboarding.failuresBody: Expected Items that were not found are listed here as extraction failures — surfaced, not hidden — separately from Items that are legitimately absent.

input.customTitle: Look up or submit a filing
input.textPlaceholder: Paste filing text — or copy a result's canonical text, delete/reorder an Item, and resubmit to watch the validator react…
input.needTokenHint: Enter the access token above to run this lookup.
input.elapsed: {{seconds}}s — fetching + segmenting (a large filing can take up to ~2 min)

empty.start: Pick a curated demo, or enter a ticker + fiscal year or an EDGAR accession, then Extract. Results are cached by accession.

detail.absentExplain: This item is legitimately absent for this filing (template/DEI says optional, reserved, or incorporated by reference).
detail.failureExplain: This item was expected but not found — a flagged extraction failure, not a silent drop.
detail.noSourceSpan: No source span for this item (not present in the document).

boundary.ariaLabel: Source text with item boundary highlighted

failures.title: Missing items
failures.noFailures: None — every expected item was found.

indexDontGenerate.d: Each Item's text is a byte range pointing into the original filing, not text a model rewrote. What you see is the source, reproducible character-for-character.

calibratedConfidence.d: A score where Items marked, say, 90% are correct about 90% of the time — backed by a statistical guarantee, not a raw model hunch.

tabInspect.d: Run the extractor on one filing and examine every Item, its confidence, and the checks behind it.

tabEval.d: The aggregate quality report across many filings, plus the cases that remain difficult. It grades the output, not any single run.

curatedDemo.d: Pre-selected example filings you can run with no token, to see the app work end-to-end.

accessToken.d: A shared key the operator gives you. Required to look up or submit your own filings — it gates the EDGAR-fetching backend.

ticker.d: The stock symbol (e.g. AAPL). Combined with a fiscal year, it looks up that company's 10-K on EDGAR.

accession.d: EDGAR's unique ID for one specific filing (e.g. 0000320193-24-000123). Use it to fetch an exact document.

pasteUpload.d: Feed raw filing text directly — e.g. delete or reorder an Item and resubmit to watch the validator react.

formatEra.d: Which filing technology the document uses: SGML (older plain-text filings), HTML, or iXBRL (inline-tagged data). Extraction handles all three; the era says which rules applied.

coverage.d: The share of the filing's characters assigned to a located Item. Low coverage means part of the document went unaccounted for — a sign segmentation missed something.

present.d: The Item was found and its text located in the source.

absent.d: The Item is expected to be missing for this filing — reserved, optional for a smaller company, or incorporated by reference. Not an error.

failure.d: The Item was expected but could not be located. It is flagged, never silently dropped.

confHigh.d: Multiple checks agree; safe to trust with minimal review.

confMedium.d: Mostly consistent, but one signal is weak; a quick look is wise.

confLow.d: Checks disagree or evidence is thin; review before relying on it.

confUnscored.d: No confidence was computed — e.g. the Item is absent, so there is nothing to score.

provenance.d: Where this Item's answer came from: which extractors produced it and which validation checks it passed or failed. Lets you audit a result instead of trusting it blindly.

checks.d: Independent validations run on the Item. A passed check is evidence for; a failed check (e.g. boundary_xcheck) is evidence against, and pulls the Item toward needing review.

boundaryDisagreement.d: Two independent methods disagree on where an Item starts or ends. A wrong boundary on an otherwise-present Item is the main silent-failure mode, so it is flagged red.

charRange.d: The start and end character positions of this Item in the canonical text. The boundary viewer highlights that exact span.

boundaryViewer.d: Shows the canonical source text with this Item's char range highlighted, so you can see exactly what was captured and where it begins and ends.

needsReview.d: At least one check failed or a value looks implausible, so a human should look. The drivers list the specific reasons.

item8Xbrl.d: An independent check: Item 8 (financial statements) is cross-verified against the filing's structured XBRL data. "Oracle" means a ground-truth source independent of our own extractor.

roundTrip.d: Reassembling every Item's char range and checking it rebuilds the original document exactly. It proves the Items cover the source with no gaps or overlaps — it does not prove the labels are right.

structural.d: Basic rules every valid result must satisfy — ranges ordered, non-overlapping, within bounds. A broken invariant is a definite bug, not a judgment call.

escalation.d: The last resort. Only flagged or low-confidence boundaries are sent to an LLM, which may only pick an Item ID and a line reference — it cannot rewrite text. The common case never reaches it.

escalationTokens.d: How much the LLM tier ran: number of calls and input/output tokens. Zero means the layered extractor resolved everything without paying for an LLM.
